task_completion.description: Create two simulated 'Pulse Oximeter' devices in OpenICE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE interface should be visible with two separate 'Pulse Oximeter' devices added to the simulated device list.
The 'Vital Signs' application window must be open and visible on the screen, confirming data flow from the devices.

task_completion.description: Investigate device details to find the unique UUID for each device.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tooltip, window title, or configuration panel must be visible on screen displaying the internal Unique Device Identifier (UDI/UUID) for the pulse oximeters.

task_completion.description: Create a JSON mapping file at '/home/ga/Desktop/device_map.json' distinguishing the two monitors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal must show the creation or content of 'device_map.json' on the Desktop, containing the keys 'monitor_1_uuid' and 'monitor_2_uuid' with corresponding UUID values.

task_completion.description: Take a screenshot of the view where IDs were found and save it to '/home/ga/Desktop/id_evidence.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'id_evidence.png' must be visible on the Desktop or in a file manager, or a screenshot tool must be shown saving the file to this exact path.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenICE GUI to create devices and launch applications.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenICE graphical user interface being used (e.g., clicking buttons to add devices), rather than just headless terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent extracted real UUIDs from the application rather than hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UUIDs entered into the 'device_map.json' file must visually match the UUIDs displayed in the OpenICE tooltips or configuration panels shown in the screenshots.

integrity.description: Agent actually captured a screenshot of the OpenICE interface for the evidence file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use a screenshot utility (like gnome-screenshot) to capture the screen, rather than creating a blank image or renaming an unrelated file to 'id_evidence.png'.
